title: Advection-Dispersion Transport Inversion
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: Advection-Dispersion Transport Inversion: ADE inversion: recover solute source concentration history from downgradient breakthrough curves. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (M.ade.advection_dispersion…); recovery is posed as a linear_inverse problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~200; velocity_field_uncertainty, dispersion_coefficient_uncertainty set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.world_state_x: source concentration history S(t) at source location x_s
E.observation_y: breakthrough curves C(x_obs, t) at N_monitoring wells

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 1D_concentration_source is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 200); velocity_field_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: source concentration RMSE < 10% with well-constrained transport params. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~200. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |velocity_field_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N_wells * N_timesteps * N_source_bins) for design matrix per iteration
